Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.

Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.

Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Key observations after SPACE:
1. The white bar changed from [[28, 20], [31, 20]] (px=4, vertical strip) to [[28, 16], [28, 20], [31, 20], [31, 16]] (px=16, a hollow rectangle!)
2. This white bar is now at the same position as where the left frame was (rows 28-31, cols 16-19)!
3. The left frame is still gone (broken)
4. The white bar now looks like a frame outline - it replaced the charcoal frame with a white frame!
So SPACE converts the broken frame (charcoal) into a white frame! This is the key mechanic:
1. Move to a frame's location to break it (charcoal frame disappears)
2. Press SPACE to convert it to a white frame (permanent change)
3. Repeat for all 3 frames
Now I need to:
1. Break the center frame (move to its location)
2. Press SPACE to convert it to white
3. Break the right frame (move to its location)
4. Press SPACE to convert it to white
Player is at rows 28-31, cols 21-23. Center frame is at rows 32-35, cols 32-35.
To reach center frame: need to move DOWN (to rows 32-35) and RIGHT (to cols 32-35).
From rows 28-31 to rows 32-35: DOWN moves +4 rows, so one DOWN gets to rows 32-35.
From cols 21-23 to cols 32-35: RIGHT moves +4 cols, so about 3 RIGHTs gets to cols 33-35.
Let me go to the center frame.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# SPACE converted the broken left frame to a WHITE frame!
# White frame at [[28, 16], [28, 20], [31, 20], [31, 16]] - same position as left frame was
# Mechanic: break frame by entering its space, then SPACE to convert to white
# Need to do this for center and right frames too.

# Player at rows 28-31, cols 21-23
# Center frame at rows 32-35, cols 32-35
# Move DOWN then RIGHT to reach center frame

action(['DOWN', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT'])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"RIGHT": "Moves player +4 cols", "UP": "Moves player -3 rows", "LEFT": "Moves player -3 cols", "DOWN": "Moves player +4 rows", "SPACE": "Converts broken frame to white frame"}, "object_taxonomy": {"charcoal_frame": "Breaks when player enters space", "white_frame": "Permanent - converted from charcoal via SPACE", "blue_center": "Target inside frame", "green_object": "Player", "pink_bar": "HUD timer", "charcoal_hud_bar": "Step counter"}, "hud_map": {"bottom_row": "HUD"}, "win_pattern": "Break all frames then SPACE each to convert to white", "level_log": {"level_1": "Left frame converted to white"}, "cross_level_notes": ""}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Level 1: Left frame converted to white. Going for center frame.", "goal_model": "Convert all 3 frames to white", "recent_findings": ["SPACE converts broken charcoal frame to white frame"], "open_questions": [], "current_plan": "Break center frame, SPACE, then right frame, SPACE", "strategy_log": [], "failed_probes": []}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
